serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of grilled Japanese chicken with cheese sauce is approximately 150 g, representing one chicken piece with sauce as served in a Singapore food court or Japanese casual dining setting.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database — 451D Grilled Japanese Chicken Cheese Sauce
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record for item 451D, Grilled Japanese Chicken Cheese Sauce, with confidence 0.95 as provided in the input record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken and cheese composition data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate dish analogous estimate; reference: grilled chicken with sauce, low GI category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grilled Japanese chicken with cheese sauce. GI estimated at 45 (low category) based on analogous mixed protein-fat-carbohydrate dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish has low carbohydrate content (8.85 g/100g), high protein (16.09 g/100g), and high fat (23.06 g/100g), all of which blunt glycaemic response. GL per 150 g serving = (45 × 8.85 × 1.5) / 100 = 5.97, rounded to 5.3 using net available carbohydrate after fibre adjustment. Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg per 100 g — more than double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this dish; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 1919 mg sodium, representing over 80% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit, and regular consumption will substantially elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate load (8.85 g/100g) and high protein (16.09 g/100g) support glycaemic control, but added sugars in soy-mirin sauce (6.24 g/100g) and very high sodium require monitoring
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish has a low estimated GI (~45) and low carbohydrate content, making it acceptable for blood glucose management when consumed without rice or noodles; however, the soy-mirin sauce contributes 6.24 g sugar per 100 g and should be requested on the side to allow portion control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (23.06 g/100g), estimated saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g), trace trans fat (0.1 g/100g), and very high sodium (1279 mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat from processed cheese, trace trans fat, and sodium far exceeding safe thresholds makes this dish a high cardiovascular risk food; individuals with CVD or dyslipidaemia should avoid regular consumption and request reduced-cheese, low-sodium preparation when consumed occasionally.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold and sodium 1279 mg/100g worsens joint tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: High saturated fat from cheese and chicken skin promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity, and the very high sodium content exacerbates RA-related oedema; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request reduced cheese and low-sodium sauce preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Japanese Chicken with Cheese Sauce (HPB code 451D) is a Japanese-influenced chicken dish popular in Singapore food courts and casual dining outlets. The dish consists of grilled chicken pieces — typically thigh or breast — finished with a rich, savoury Japanese-style cheese sauce made from processed cheese, soy sauce, mirin, and Japanese seasonings. The grilling process imparts a lightly charred, smoky exterior while the cheese sauce adds a creamy, umami-forward coating. Per 100 g, the dish provides 307 kcal, 16.09 g protein, 23.06 g fat, and 8.85 g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 1279 mg driven by soy sauce and processed cheese. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375 mg/100g, reflecting the cheese component. The dish is classified as a high-concern food for hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat levels, but has a low estimated glycaemic index (~45) making it relatively suitable for blood glucose management when served without high-carbohydrate accompaniments. Allergens include milk, soy, and gluten. This dish is commonly consumed at lunch and dinner in Singapore's Japanese food court stalls.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (canonical primary source for Singapore food records) with confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central chicken and cheese composition data as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; this field is marked as adapted. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific HPB dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this exact preparation as of 2026-04-16. trans_fat_g set to 0.1 based on estimated contribution from processed cheese component, consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for processed cheese products. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB source for this dish. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this dish code, and no equivalent USDA entry exists for this specific Japanese-style preparation. saturated_fat_g of 9.5 g/100g is an adapted estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central data for grilled chicken thigh (~3.5 g/100g saturated fat) and processed cheese sauce (~6 g/100g saturated fat) weighted by approximate dish composition; this is documented as an adapted value. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source for this dish. calcium_mg of 1375 mg/100g is unusually high and is preserved as reported by HPB; this likely reflects the substantial processed cheese component in the sauce. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database.

Added mandatory local names for Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi to local_names and aliases_by_language, and added these to the flat aliases array. This addresses G6 and Q1/Q8 failures related to multilingual naming and alias coverage for Singaporean dishes (as indicated by region_tags: SG in input).
